openAlexDesc: OpenAlex is a fully open catalog of the global research system, providing free access to hundreds of millions of scholarly works, authors, institutions, and more. It serves as an open alternative to proprietary services like Scopus and Web of Science.
topicsDesc: Topics are concept-level labels automatically assigned to scholarly works by OpenAlex. Each topic represents a specific research concept or area, allowing researchers to explore and discover papers based on conceptual relevance rather than just keywords.
conceptsDesc: Concepts are abstract ideas that scholarly works are about. OpenAlex indexes about 65,000 concepts organized in a hierarchical tree with 19 root-level disciplines and six layers of descendants, from general fields like Computer Science to highly specialized sub-concepts.
topicFeature1: 4,516 unique topics across all research areas
conceptFeature1: 65,073 unique concepts across all research areas
topicFeature3: Hierarchical organization from broad to specific
conceptFeature3: 19 root disciplines with six layers of descendants
topicsTreeDesc: Topics are organized in a 4-level hierarchy:
conceptsTreeDesc: Concepts are organized in a hierarchical tree with 19 root disciplines and about 65,000 concepts across six levels:
hierarchyDomain: Domain — Broad research areas (e.g. Physical Sciences)
hierarchyConceptsLevel0: 🌐 Domain — Top-level disciplines (e.g. Computer Science, Medicine)
howToUseDesc: Browse the hierarchical tree structure or search for specific concepts by name. Click the OpenAlex button on any concept to view related scholarly works.
usageTip1: Click a node to expand and explore sub-categories
usageTip2: Use search to filter and highlight matching concepts
usageTip3: View works count and topic count for each node
worksCountTitle: Total scholarly works in this category
